fields.summary: Federation: YARN running app web can't be unable to connect, because AppMaster can't redirect to the right address.

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description:
I setup a yarn federation cluster, I can't connect the running app web, but the completed and accepted app's web works.

So I think need two step: 

(a) make proxy server support federation.

(b) make router support proxy server.

Though it is a not difficult problem, but not easy to describe the problem. So I submit a document YARN-10775-design-doc.001.pdf to explain this.

 

If standalone proxyserver is enable, after step (a), the problem is solved.

If standalone proxyserver is disable, after step (a) and (b), we use router as web proxy server, so we hide the cluster info for client, I think it is reasonable.

fields.summary: Federation:We can't access the AM page while using federation

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.summary: Make proxy server support YARN federation.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

comments.body:
YARN-10786 describe same problem, but have two problem: 

(1) Do not hide the cluster info for client. If admin add a cluster, all client must know and update their config. I think this break the yarn federation design.

(2) Can't support standalone proxyserver mode.

[~inigoiri]  [~snemeth] [~ayushsaxena] [~bteke] [~zhuqi] 

Can you help me review this document?

And I split two task YARN-11153 and YARN-11154 to solve this problem.

Thanks [~zhengchenyu] for the proposal.
It looks reasonable.
I'll go through YARN-11153.
Maybe you want to make this one an umbrella and have the other 2 JIRAs under this one.

Thanks a lot for the detailed design documentation, the documentation is very clear.

1.I personally think that resourcemanager should not assume the role of proxyserver,
I feel that this operation should be implemented on the router, the router should know the rm list, but the rm should not know the router list, each rm should be able to run independently.

2.I don't think there is a problem with letting the client know the location of rms, just like viewfs, the client will know the location of all namenodes. The advantage of letting the client know the cluster list is that if the router fails, it can traverse the SubCluster list to ensure that the job can be submitted and the cluster as a whole will not be unavailable.

3.Thank you very much for your design doc, but can you provide the documentation for the version supported by router.

4.Why need standalone proxyserver?

[~slfan1989] 

Answer 1：

This operation implement in proxyserver. Nowadays, proxyserver have two form:

(1) standalone proxy server. (2) embedded in resourcemanager. 

In next subtask YARN-11154, I will setup embedded proxyserver in router, it is reasonable way indeed. But YARN-11154 is blocked by YARN-11153. I must wait YARN-11153 merge to trunk. Then I will continue YARN-11154 . (You could see the picture in Chapter 3 How to fix?)

In this patch, rm does not need to know router list, proxyserver embeded in rm also does not need to know router list. Proxyserver need to know federation state.

Note: proxyserver embeded in rm is not final version.  proxyserver embeded in router is final version.

 

Answer 2:

Your analogy is not proper, why not dfsrouter. If just like viewfs, we could set strategy in client side, then there are no need for us to setup router.

I think router is designed as rm in client view, so implement rm's function (except ApplicationMasterProtocol), means router need to implement proxy. Just like hdfs client regard dfsrouter as namenode.

 

Answer 3:

This PR is base on trunk. I have verified this function in our cluster, our internal version base on v3.2.1。

 

Answer 4:

Standalone proxyserver exists indeed. I think we can't remove the module. For security reason, if appmaster return a danger proxy url, router or rm call danger url is not secure.

Though I does not use standalone proxyserver, we must take into account. I think someone which have high security demand may use standalone proxyserver.

[~zhengchenyu] Thank you for your answer, but I need help answering the following questions

In Yarn-Federation （[https://hadoop.apache.org/docs/stable/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-site/Federation.html]）

1.We can see that the client finds the RM through the router. I don't think it needs to be adjusted in the RM. According to your design, if the number of routers increases, all RMs need to be restarted to let the proxyserver know the location of the router. seems unreasonable.

2.Why does the proxyserver need to know the list of routers? This seems to be inconsistent with the design of yarn-federation？

3.For Answer 2, I mean, I think YARN-10786's solution is lightweight and effective, your rebuttal is not very accurate.

4.For Answer 4, Why do you need a standalone proxyserver? If the user does not enable federation, no proxyserver is needed. If the user enables federation, can't you use the router directly? For security issues, I see that there are already chapters in the yarn-federation design document. If AM really returns a dangerous url, the risk should be borne by the router, not RM.And as far as the current code implementation is concerned, there is no processing logic for dangerous urls.

If I want to get in touch with you offline, can you provide me with a simple way to contact you

[~slfan1989] 

I think you need read my answer and document again, seems does not catch my idea. I think if you know why we need standalone proxy server, some question will be solved.

If you are puzzled about my document, you can just see picture in "Chapter 3 How to Fix". Most of the document is the problem, not design. Only Chapter 3 is design and final version. YARN-11153 is just  first stage. Then we must make YARN-11154 done.

email: zhengchenyu16@gmail.com

[~zhengchenyu] , I probably understand what you mean. It seems reasonable, but I still have the question just now. If subcluser or router is added, all proxyservers in the cluster need to be restarted. Is this design a bit heavy?

Also, I don't think there is any need to improve rm's proxyserver. Can't these operations be done directly on the router? If it is done on the router, no matter whether the router increases or the rm increases, there will be no particularly large cost.

Or explain it this way, I feel that the proxyserver of rm should not know the information of other rms.

Thank you so much for the design, it made me think a lot, it was very helpful!

[~zhengchenyu] If possible, I would like to see your design of the router's proxyserver, so that we can discuss it better. Let us understand your design from a global perspective, maybe my understanding is not accurate, I always think that these operations can be Modify directly on the router, there is no need to modify it on rm, thanks again.

In our cluster, the final version, I don't regard rm as proxy server, I regard router as proxy server. RM does not need to know the information of other rms. This PR is just the first step a.

Embedded proxy server in router will done in YARN-11154.

Why I split two step? Because after step a, we solve the problem in standalone proxyserver mode. After step a and b, we solve the problem without standalone proxyserver.

If subcluster is added, no need to restart proxyserver. I use FederationStateStoreFacade to get new subcluster information. (no router, proxyserver does not know router info.)

[~zhengchenyu] I understand what you mean, I recommend going directly to YARN-11154, YARN-11153 as an intermediate state, the value is limited, I am looking forward to your design in YARN-11154.

As I understand it, once YARN-11154 is complete, YARN-11153 has no value, is that what it means? Because YARN-11154 solves two problems a and b.

If the router or rm is offline, how can the proxyserver perceive it accurately?

Thanks for review and suggestion. Welcome continue to review and give me some suggesstion.

 YARN-11153  should done before  YARN-11154. YARN-11153 is meaningful. Because If router support proxyserver, but proxyserver can't know multi subcluster information, it is meaningless. YARN-11153  make proxyserver know the multi-subcluster information.

[~zhengchenyu] I have understood what you mean, it is a good design for me, but I still hope to provide the design doc of YARN-11154 first, and describe how the router uses proxyserver, because YARN-11153 and YARN-11154 are essentially the same thing , it's better to complete it in the same pr, this split way makes me have some misunderstandings.Thanks again!
[~slfan1989] In fact, the picture of chapter 3 have include YARN-11154 . For review easily, I will submit a draft PR for YARN-11154 Later.
[~zhengchenyu] ok, looking forward to seeing YARN-11154.

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open
